canonical_definition: CEval is presented in the provided literature as a comprehensive evaluation benchmark for the Chinese language. According to its single available definition, it covers "diverse academic and professional domains" and is designed "to assess general knowledge." Across the surveyed papers, CEval is operationalized to measure several different capabilities. The most frequently documented application is the measurement of `Faithfulness`, a use case appearing in two papers. Additionally, individual studies employ CEval to evaluate `Language understanding` and `Text generation`. One source snippet situates CEval alongside other evaluation tools like MMLU and GSM, suggesting its use in similar contexts.
papers: OmniEval: An Omnidirectional and AutomaticRAGEvaluation Benchmark in Financial Domain
Retrieval Enhanced Feedback via In-context Neural Error-book
Step-level Verifier-guided Hybrid Test-Time Scaling for Large Language Models
